ExpectedTimetable.name: Expected Timetable
ExpectedTimetable.requirements:
Draft in sponsor-counsel working draft mode, not as a fully complete final filing copy.

Role: Present the IPO timetable as a precision timetable and compliance control schedule.

Required structure:
- Lead-in note that the timetable may change and announcements will be published.
- Table or bullet timeline with event, date, time, and note fields.
- Include application opening and closing, pricing, results and allotment, share certificate validity or CCASS crediting, and expected trading commencement if supported.
- State that all dates and times are Hong Kong time unless otherwise stated.
- Include severe weather or extreme conditions wording if supported.

Controls:
- Treat this as a factual timing section, not marketing copy.
- Keep every event line even if incomplete; for missing dates or times use [Information not provided in the documents].
- Add [[AI:XREF|to=GlobalOfferingStructure]] where timetable items depend on offering mechanics.
- If application procedures are not separately available, add [[AI:VERIFY|Confirm application channel timing, severe weather arrangements, and results publication process.]].

Contents.name: Contents
Contents.requirements:
Draft in sponsor-counsel working draft mode, not as a fully complete final filing copy.

Role: Provide a hierarchical table of contents for the prospectus.

Required structure:
- List the front matter, risk and compliance chapters, business and financial chapters, offering chapters, and appendices if supported.
- Use section titles exactly as they appear in the generated document.
- Show page numbers or section references; if pagination is unavailable, keep the entry and mark the page field as [Information not provided in the documents].

Controls:
- No orphan sections: if a section is listed here it must appear elsewhere, and vice versa.
- Add [[AI:XREF|to=all section headings]] or equivalent notes wherever heading alignment must be checked.
- Add [[AI:VERIFY|Final pagination and heading consistency check required before filing.]].

Summary.name: Summary
Summary.requirements:
Draft in sponsor-counsel working draft mode, not as a fully complete final filing copy.

Role: Provide a balanced executive summary of the issuer and the offering.

Required structure:
- Opening purpose statement explaining that the summary should be read with the full prospectus.
- Business and issuer overview.
- Industry and market context only if supported.
- Key operating and financial information, preferably in concise tables if the data supports it.
- Material strengths stated neutrally and only if evidenced.
- Key risks with [[AI:XREF|to=Risk Factors]].
- Offering overview and recent developments if supported.

Controls:
- No marketing hype, no superlatives unless tightly scoped and supported.
- Any rank, market share, CAGR, or leadership statement must carry [[AI:CITE|source=...; scope=...; date=...; metric=...]] or be omitted.
- Do not introduce a risk theme here unless it is also reflected in Risk Factors.
- Numeric KPIs must match Business and Financial Information or be replaced with [Information not provided in the documents].
- Do not quantify future profitability, margins, or valuation outcomes.

Definitions.name: Definitions
Definitions.requirements:
Draft in sponsor-counsel working draft mode, not as a fully complete final filing copy.

Role: Provide the formal Definitions section as a dictionary-style list.

Required structure:
- Alphabetical list of defined terms.
- Include company entities, key people where defined, regulators, legal and financial abbreviations, and recurring deal terms if supported.
- Maintain prospectus-style precise wording rather than narrative prose.

Controls:
- Do not invent definitions for terms not supported by the documents.
- Avoid definition creep: define only terms that are actually used or clearly need standardised meaning.
- Keep standard jurisdiction definitions consistent across the document.
- Add [[AI:VERIFY|Confirm each defined term matches actual usage throughout the draft.]] where helpful.

Glossary.name: Glossary of Technical Terms
Glossary.requirements:
Draft in sponsor-counsel working draft mode, not as a fully complete final filing copy.

Role: Explain technical or industry-specific terms in plain language for investors.

Required structure:
- Alphabetical list of technical terms or acronyms.
- Each entry should be concise, plain-language, and non-promotional.
- Include only technical concepts that are relevant to the business and used elsewhere in the draft.

Controls:
- Do not duplicate legal or entity definitions that belong in Definitions.
- Avoid circular or self-referential explanations.
- Risk-sensitive technical terms should carry [[AI:XREF|to=Business]], [[AI:XREF|to=Regulation]], or [[AI:XREF|to=Risk Factors]] where appropriate.
- For missing technical definitions keep the term and use [Information not provided in the documents].

ForwardLooking.name: Forward-Looking Statements
ForwardLooking.requirements:
Draft in sponsor-counsel working draft mode, not as a fully complete final filing copy.

Role: Provide a formal cautionary disclaimer for forward-looking statements.

Required structure:
- Explain what forward-looking statements are.
- State that such statements are based on assumptions and currently available information.
- Warn that actual results may differ materially.
- Caution investors not to place undue reliance on such statements.
- State that the statements speak only as of the date of the prospectus and no update obligation arises except as required by law.
- Add [[AI:XREF|to=Risk Factors]].

Controls:
- Use formal legal language and neutral examples only.
- Do not include express or implied profit forecasts, margin forecasts, or dated profitability claims.
- If company-specific future plans are not evidenced, do not invent them; use generic safe-harbour wording and [Information not provided in the documents] where necessary.

RiskFactors.name: Risk Factors
RiskFactors.requirements:
Draft in sponsor-counsel working draft mode, not as a fully complete final filing copy.

Role: Present material applicant-specific risks in a frank, structured, and disclosure-focused manner.

Required structure:
- Group risks by category, such as business and industry, regulation and compliance, financial condition, technology or data, and offering or shareholder risks.
- Use one principal risk per sub-heading.
- Under each sub-heading explain the factual cause, how the risk could adversely affect the issuer or investors, and quantification if supported by the documents.
- If the impact cannot be quantified from the documents, say so directly rather than guessing.

Controls:
- No mitigation language inside the same risk paragraph; place any mitigation in other sections and use [[AI:XREF|to=Business]] or [[AI:XREF|to=Regulation]] where appropriate.
- No vague unknown-risks boilerplate.
- For technology issuers, include supported risks relating to commercialisation, data privacy and cybersecurity, IP ownership, model performance or safety, third-party dependency, WVR, or 18C where applicable.
- Unsupported required risks should remain as headings with [Information not provided in the documents] plus [[AI:VERIFY|Obtain issuer-specific facts and quantification for this risk.]].

Waivers.name: Waivers from Strict Compliance with Listing Rules (Waivers and Exemptions)
Waivers.requirements:
Draft in sponsor-counsel working draft mode, not as a fully complete final filing copy.

Role: Disclose any waivers or exemptions from strict compliance with Listing Rules or prospectus requirements.

Required structure:
- For each waiver use the sequence: relevant rule, rule requirement, applicant fact pattern, waiver or exemption granted, conditions, and investor relevance.
- Cite rule numbers and titles where supported.
- If no waiver is disclosed in the documents, say so expressly.

Controls:
- Do not say a waiver was granted unless the documents support that conclusion.
- If a waiver signals structural or compliance risk, add [[AI:XREF|to=Risk Factors]].
- Add [[AI:VERIFY|Obtain Exchange approval evidence and confirm compliance with any conditions.]] where details are incomplete.
- Keep unsupported line items as [Information not provided in the documents] rather than inferring the waiver package.

InfoProspectus.name: Information about this Prospectus and the Global Offering
InfoProspectus.requirements:
Draft in sponsor-counsel working draft mode, not as a fully complete final filing copy.

Role: Provide key reliance, distribution, and offering-notice language for the prospectus and the Global Offering.

Required structure:
- Reliance-only-on-this-prospectus wording.
- No unauthorised information wording.
- Territorial restrictions and no-public-offer-outside-permitted-jurisdictions wording if supported.
- Listing, trading, and CCASS or settlement context if supported.
- Currency and exchange rate note if relevant.
- Website-not-part-of-the-prospectus wording where website references appear.
- Directors' responsibility wording if supported.

Controls:
- Use formal explanatory prose, not marketing language.
- Do not imply a profit forecast, valuation certainty, or offering completion certainty.
- Add [[AI:XREF|to=Risk Factors]] and [[AI:XREF|to=GlobalOfferingStructure]] where helpful.
- Missing notices should remain visible as [Information not provided in the documents] plus [[AI:VERIFY|Confirm standard reliance, territorial restriction, and website-not-part wording.]].

DirectorsParties.name: Directors and Parties Involved in the Global Offering
DirectorsParties.requirements:
Draft in sponsor-counsel working draft mode, not as a fully complete final filing copy.

Role: Provide a directory of directors and the professional parties involved in the transaction.

Required structure:
- Directors with title and any address or nationality fields if supported.
- Professional parties such as sponsors, coordinators, bookrunners, lead managers, legal advisers, reporting accountants or auditors, valuers, compliance advisers, receiving banks, and registrars if supported.
- Use a list or table rather than narrative prose.

Controls:
- Names must match the source documents exactly.
- Do not invent roles, firm names, or addresses.
- Add [[AI:XREF|to=Underwriting]] where party names must reconcile across sections.
- Add [[AI:VERIFY|Confirm official names and appointments against engagement letters or formal appointment records.]] when any entry is incomplete.

CorporateInfo.name: Corporate Information
CorporateInfo.requirements:
Draft in sponsor-counsel working draft mode, not as a fully complete final filing copy.

Role: Present key corporate particulars as a reference block.

Required structure:
- Jurisdiction of incorporation and registered office.
- Principal place of business and contact particulars if supported.
- Website reference with a statement that the website does not form part of the prospectus.
- Company secretary, authorised representatives, compliance adviser, registrar, stock code, and similar particulars if supported.

Controls:
- Use structured list or table format.
- Do not infer missing corporate particulars.
- Add [[AI:LPD|refresh=corporate particulars and officers]] for information likely to change before filing.
- Add [[AI:VERIFY|Confirm incorporation, registered office, company secretary, and registrar details from corporate records.]] when required.

Regulation.name: Regulation (Regulatory Overview)
Regulation.requirements:
Draft in sponsor-counsel working draft mode, not as a fully complete final filing copy.

Role: Summarise the principal laws, regulations, licences, approvals, and regulatory practices that materially affect the business.

Required structure:
- Organise by regulatory topic, such as licensing and approvals, data privacy and cybersecurity, internet information services, foreign ownership restrictions, IP, consumer or product regulation, and other sector-specific rules as supported.
- For each topic state the main rule set, the relevant regulator, the core requirement, and the issuer's compliance status if supported.
- Mention material incidents, pending applications, or regulatory changes if evidenced.

Controls:
- Cross-reference Business and Risk Factors using [[AI:XREF|to=Business]] and [[AI:XREF|to=Risk Factors]] where relevant.
- For technology issuers, do not omit data lifecycle controls, personal data categories, or material data incidents if they are supported by the documents.
- Do not downplay regulatory risk or state legal conclusions without support.
- Add [[AI:VERIFY|Obtain legal opinions, licence evidence, filing records, and incident logs.]] where required.

IndustryOverview.name: Industry Overview
IndustryOverview.requirements:
Draft in sponsor-counsel working draft mode, not as a fully complete final filing copy.

Role: Provide a neutral overview of the industry and markets in which the issuer operates.

Required structure:
- Define the industry or relevant market segments.
- Present market size, growth, demand drivers, and competitive landscape if supported.
- Include concentration, ranking, share, barriers to entry, or outlook only if supported by sourced materials.
- Use tables or concise prose as the data allows.

Controls:
- Every market statistic, projection, ranking, share, or CAGR must carry [[AI:CITE|source=...; scope=...; date=...; metric=...]].
- Do not present unsourced leadership claims, estimated growth, or third-party projections as issuer facts.
- If sourcing metadata is incomplete, keep the statement as [Information not provided in the documents] and add [[AI:VERIFY|Obtain full third-party source, scope, metric, and date.]].
- Add [[AI:XREF|to=Business]] and [[AI:XREF|to=Risk Factors]] where competitive or market statements connect to company disclosure.

HistoryReorg.name: History, Reorganization, and Corporate Structure
HistoryReorg.requirements:
Draft in sponsor-counsel working draft mode, not as a fully complete final filing copy.

Role: Explain the issuer's historical development, any pre-IPO reorganisation, and the resulting group structure.

Required structure:
- Chronological company history and major milestones.
- Reorganisation steps undertaken for listing purposes.
- Current group structure, including principal entities, jurisdictions, ownership relationships, and diagram references if needed.
- Pre- and post-listing ownership context if supported.

Controls:
- Do not invent dates, legal steps, ownership percentages, or entity relationships.
- Keep a clean chain-of-title approach and add [[AI:VERIFY|Obtain board resolutions, reorganisation agreements, share transfer documents, and filings.]].
- Add [[AI:XREF|to=ShareCapital]] and [[AI:XREF|to=SubstantialShareholders]] where structural information must reconcile.
- If a structural step is required but unsupported, preserve the sub-heading and use [Information not provided in the documents].

Business.name: Business
Business.requirements:
Draft in sponsor-counsel working draft mode, not as a fully complete final filing copy.

Role: Provide a detailed, evidence-based description of the issuer's operations.

Required structure:
- Business overview.
- Products and services.
- Business model and revenue streams.
- Customers, suppliers, operations, facilities, and key operational metrics if supported.
- Technology, research and development, and IP if supported.
- Competition and market positioning only if supported.
- Compliance-sensitive operations, including data privacy, cybersecurity, internet information services, or other sector-specific matters if supported.
- Growth strategy only where current plans are supported and carefully qualified.

Controls:
- No hype, no unsourced leadership claims, and no absolute performance statements.
- For technology issuers, include supported disclosure on personal data categories, data lifecycle controls, material incidents, key agreements, and IP ownership or licensing.
- Do not convert strategy into a profit forecast or certainty of future success.
- Add [[AI:XREF|to=Regulation]] and [[AI:XREF|to=Risk Factors]] where risk-sensitive operational matters appear.
- Add [[AI:VERIFY|Obtain key customer, supplier, R&D, IP, privacy, and incident evidence where incomplete.]] as needed.

ContractualArrangements.name: Contractual Arrangements (Variable Interest Entities)
ContractualArrangements.requirements:
Draft in sponsor-counsel working draft mode, not as a fully complete final filing copy.

Role: Explain contractual arrangements or VIE structures if the issuer uses them.

Required structure:
- Regulatory background creating the need for contractual arrangements.
- Identification of the relevant entities and parties.
- Summary of the principal contracts and how they are intended to confer control and economic benefits.
- Brief explanation of the main risks and uncertainties.
- Counsel or enforceability statements only if supported.

Controls:
- If no VIE or contractual arrangement is disclosed, state [Not applicable - no VIE or contractual arrangement disclosed in the documents].
- Do not invent the existence of contracts, legal conclusions, or regulatory barriers.
- Add [[AI:XREF|to=Regulation]] and [[AI:XREF|to=Risk Factors]] for consistency.
- Add [[AI:VERIFY|Obtain executed VIE agreements, PRC counsel opinions, and supporting regulatory materials.]] where relevant.

ControllingShareholders.name: Relationship with Our Controlling Shareholders
ControllingShareholders.requirements:
Draft in sponsor-counsel working draft mode, not as a fully complete final filing copy.

Role: Disclose the identity of controlling shareholders, their influence, and the arrangements intended to preserve issuer independence.

Required structure:
- Identify controlling shareholders and their shareholding or voting influence if supported.
- Explain relationship to the Group and any actual or potential conflicts.
- Set out operational, management, and financial independence measures if supported.
- Describe non-competition or governance arrangements if supported.
- Cross-reference related dealings where relevant.

Controls:
- If WVR applies, highlight the control-risk narrative and any divergence between voting power and economic ownership.
- Do not give comfort statements about independence unless the documents support them.
- Add [[AI:XREF|to=RiskFactors]] and [[AI:XREF|to=ConnectedTransactions]] where relevant.
- Add [[AI:VERIFY|Obtain register, shareholder agreements, governance policies, and non-competition deeds.]] where necessary.

ConnectedTransactions.name: Connected Transactions
ConnectedTransactions.requirements:
Draft in sponsor-counsel working draft mode, not as a fully complete final filing copy.

Role: Disclose connected persons and connected transactions in a compliance-oriented format.

Required structure:
- Identify connected persons if supported.
- Describe historical connected transactions, continuing connected transactions, and any basis for annual caps if supported.
- State internal control procedures, exemptions, or waivers only if supported.
- Use tables where monetary data or annual caps are available.

Controls:
- Do not invent connected-person status, cap amounts, or waiver status.
- If no connected transactions are disclosed, say so expressly.
- Add [[AI:XREF|to=FinancialInfo]] where transaction economics or dependency must reconcile with the financial discussion.
- Add [[AI:VERIFY|Obtain contracts, pricing support, board or committee approvals, and internal control documentation.]] when details are incomplete.

DirectorsSeniorMgmt.name: Directors and Senior Management
DirectorsSeniorMgmt.requirements:
Draft in sponsor-counsel working draft mode, not as a fully complete final filing copy.

Role: Present factual profiles of directors and senior management.

Required structure:
- Separate board roles and senior management where appropriate.
- For each person include name, age if supported, title, responsibilities, relevant experience, qualifications, and other directorships or positions if supported.
- Use concise profile paragraphs or tables.

Controls:
- Keep biographies factual and avoid hype such as visionary, best-in-class, or similar promotional language.
- Do not invent educational, professional, or award details.
- Add [[AI:VERIFY|Obtain CVs, appointment letters, academic or professional credentials, and recent directorship records.]] where relevant.
- If remuneration, service contracts, or share interests are not supported, keep the relevant sub-heading with [Information not provided in the documents].

SubstantialShareholders.name: Substantial Shareholders
SubstantialShareholders.requirements:
Draft in sponsor-counsel working draft mode, not as a fully complete final filing copy.

Role: Identify substantial shareholders and present their shareholdings in a reconciled, table-based format.

Required structure:
- Present holdings as of the Latest Practicable Date and, if supported, immediately after the Global Offering.
- Include holder name, number of shares, percentage, and nature of interest.
- Identify intermediary holding vehicles or trusts where supported.

Controls:
- Use [[AI:LPD|refresh=shareholding table]] for all shareholding tables.
- Do not invent percentages, indirect ownership chains, or lock-up arrangements.
- Add [[AI:XREF|to=ShareCapital]] where reconciliation is required.
- Add [[AI:VERIFY|Obtain register of members, ownership confirmations, and group ownership charts.]] when the supporting evidence is incomplete.

ShareCapital.name: Share Capital
ShareCapital.requirements:
Draft in sponsor-counsel working draft mode, not as a fully complete final filing copy.

Role: Explain the issuer's share capital structure and any changes resulting from the offering.

Required structure:
- Authorised and issued share capital before and after the offering if supported.
- Share classes and rights attaching to each class.
- Capitalisation issue, options, RSUs, convertibles, or other dilutive instruments if supported.
- Assumptions regarding the over-allotment option or similar mechanics if supported.
- WVR-related share class disclosure if applicable and supported.

Controls:
- Do not invent share numbers, par value, percentages, class rights, or dilution effects.
- Use [[AI:LPD|refresh=share capital tables]] where current figures may change before filing.
- Add [[AI:XREF|to=ControllingShareholders]] and [[AI:XREF|to=GlobalOfferingStructure]] where capital structure must reconcile with governance and offering mechanics.
- Add [[AI:VERIFY|Perform math checks and reconcile totals, percentages, class rights, and dilution assumptions.]].

FinancialInfo.name: Financial Information
FinancialInfo.requirements:
Draft in sponsor-counsel working draft mode, not as a fully complete final filing copy.

Role: Provide historical financial information and MD&A-style narrative analysis.

Required structure:
- Overview of the track record period.
- Revenue, cost, gross profit, operating expense, and profitability discussion if supported.
- Segment or product mix discussion if supported.
- Liquidity, capital resources, indebtedness, and working capital discussion if supported.
- Balance sheet, cash flow, turnover, and key accounting policy discussion if supported.
- Non-IFRS measures only if clearly defined and reconcilable from the documents.

Controls:
- Historical facts only; do not create express or implied forecasts of future profit, loss, or margin.
- If a metric or explanation is missing, preserve the sub-heading and use [Information not provided in the documents].
- If the issuer appears to be Pre-Commercial and a working capital statement is supported, retain it and cross-reference Use of Proceeds with [[AI:XREF|to=UseOfProceeds]].
- Add [[AI:VERIFY|Obtain audited financials, accountants' report, reconciliations, and working capital support.]] where required.

UseOfProceeds.name: Future Plans and Use of Proceeds
UseOfProceeds.requirements:
Draft in sponsor-counsel working draft mode, not as a fully complete final filing copy.

Role: Explain how net proceeds are expected to be used and how the stated uses link to disclosed strategy.

Required structure:
- Short strategic overview.
- Expected gross and net proceeds baseline if supported.
- Allocation by category, with percentage and amount where supported.
- Brief implementation plan, timing, or priority notes where supported.
- Adjustment language if proceeds differ from the assumed amount and the documents support that logic.

Controls:
- Do not invent net proceeds, percentages, budgets, milestones, or deployment timing.
- Do not imply a profitability timetable or forecast.
- If the issuer appears to be Pre-Commercial, align supported uses of proceeds to commercialisation path and working capital coverage where evidenced.
- Add [[AI:XREF|to=Business]] and [[AI:XREF|to=FinancialInfo]] where strategy and liquidity need reconciliation.
- Add [[AI:VERIFY|Obtain board-approved budgets, capex plans, and net proceeds allocation support.]] when incomplete.

Underwriting.name: Underwriting
Underwriting.requirements:
Draft in sponsor-counsel working draft mode, not as a fully complete final filing copy.

Role: Summarise underwriting arrangements in formal transaction language.

Required structure:
- Underwriters and their roles.
- Scope of underwriting commitments.
- Pricing agreement mechanics if supported.
- Fees and commissions if supported.
- Lock-up arrangements, conditions precedent, termination rights, and over-allotment or stabilisation arrangements if supported.

Controls:
- Do not invent commission rates, contractual terms, termination triggers, or stabilisation details.
- Use a precise legal tone rather than narrative sales language.
- Add [[AI:XREF|to=GlobalOfferingStructure]] where the mechanics overlap.
- Add [[AI:VERIFY|Obtain underwriting agreement terms, fee disclosure support, termination clause summary, and stabilisation compliance materials.]] when required.

GlobalOfferingStructure.name: Structure of the Global Offering
GlobalOfferingStructure.requirements:
Draft in sponsor-counsel working draft mode, not as a fully complete final filing copy.

Role: Explain the structure, allocation mechanics, pricing, and settlement logic of the Global Offering.

Required structure:
- Total offer size and split between Hong Kong Public Offering and International Offering if supported.
- Clawback or reallocation mechanics and any reserved or cornerstone allocations if supported.
- Pricing process and final offer price determination logic if supported.
- Conditions for completion, refund logic, and impact of any over-allotment option if supported.
- Listing, trading, allocation result publication, and CCASS or settlement arrangements if supported.

Controls:
- Keep all assumptions explicit and do not infer board lot size, reallocation thresholds, refund timing, or settlement mechanics.
- Add [[AI:XREF|to=Underwriting]] and [[AI:XREF|to=ExpectedTimetable]] where timing and transactional mechanics must reconcile.
- If application-procedure detail is incomplete, add [[AI:VERIFY|Confirm application channel mechanics, refund process, and result publication workflow.]].
- Use [Information not provided in the documents] for unsupported numeric mechanics rather than guessing.
